findings.problem: The chapter lands the impossible-body reveal early, then keeps re-explaining the same triad of facts (fresh print age, healed scar, childhood memory residue) in later beats instead of letting each scene turn on a new consequence. That repetition blunts the Friday-queue pressure and softens escalation in a chapter whose engine should tighten from discovery into risk.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the repeat-explanation passes at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:221, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:267 so each revisit adds a different kind of pressure instead of re-proving the anomaly. Let the proxy call center deniability, the Celia scene center institutional suppression, the scan scene center proof theft, and the ending center Mira's irreversible choice.
findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:97, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:169, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:221, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:267, a reviewer can identify one new development in each passage; after the initial reveal, no later beat restates both the scar-age mismatch and the childhood-memory impossibility in full more than once.

findings.problem: The prose leans too often on the same corrective and anaphoric sentence scaffolds: 'It was not ...', 'It did not ...', 'That was not ...', and the repeated 'She thought about ...' sequence. At this frequency the pattern stops feeling like deliberate rhythm and starts reading like autopilot, flattening otherwise strong sensory writing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated formula zones at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:57, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:253, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:267 by varying the sentence motion: swap some negation-first sentences for affirmative image, action-led observation, or free-indirect pressure so the chapter's cadence keeps changing.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:23, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:221, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249, no single corrective opening pattern ('It was not', 'That was not', or 'It did not') appears more than twice across the chapter, and the decision paragraph around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:249 no longer stacks more than two consecutive sentences on the same opening syntax.

summary: Chapter 1 cleanly lands the discovery spine, keeps Mira, Celia, and the proxy assistant in distinct voice, and stays aligned with the style bible's bodily, bureaucratic pressure. It still fails on craft/prose because the anomaly is re-explained too many times and the chapter reuses the same negation-driven sentence formulas often enough to flatten momentum and texture.
